hook: AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I needed a yes or a no.
caption:
AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I needed a yes or a no.

You asked: 'Should I send this email to my boss?'

Generic AI gave you: A balanced exploration of workplace communication dynamics, the pros and cons of directness, three possible rewrites, and a reminder to 'consider your audience.'

You needed: 'Yes, send it. Cut the second paragraph.'

This is the most common AI frustration nobody talks about. It's not that the answer is wrong. It's that the answer is shaped for a person who wants to think it through — and you're a person who already thought it through.

Some people process out loud. They want the essay.

Some people process internally. By the time they're asking AI, they've already done the thinking. They just need a verdict.

Generic AI doesn't know which one you are. So it hedges. It covers its bases. It respects your autonomy by giving you six options when you wanted one.

A BootFile fixes this. It tells AI: this person is decisive, already self-aware, don't re-open the thinking — just answer the question.

Same AI. Completely different conversation.
